stimulus:
While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes:

  • In 1847, the novel Agnes Grey was published in England.
  • According to the novel's title page, the author's name was Acton Bell.
  • Acton Bell was widely assumed to be a pseudonym.
  • A pseudonym is a fake name used to conceal an author's identity.
  • A woman named Anne Brontë later revealed herself as the novel's real author.
stem: The student wants to identify the real author of Agnes Grey. Which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal?

answerOptions.content: Acton Bell, which Agnes Grey's title page indicated was the name of the novel's author, was widely assumed to be a pseudonym.
The real author of Agnes Grey was Anne Brontë, who published the novel using the pseudonym Acton Bell.
The title page of the novel Agnes Grey indicated that the author's name was Acton Bell.
A woman who had used a pseudonym to conceal her identity later revealed herself as the real author of Agnes Grey.

rationale:
Choice B is the best answer. The sentence identifies the novel's real author, explaining that Anne Brontë published the novel under the pseudonym Acton Bell.

Choice A is incorrect. The sentence explains that Acton Bell was assumed to be a pseudonym; it does not identify the novel's real author. Choice C is incorrect. The sentence specifies the pseudonym used on the novel's title page; it does not identify the novel's real author. Choice D is incorrect. While the sentence indicates that the novel's real author used a pseudonym, it does not identify that author as Anne Brontë.
